formula_note: No clean combatant-only death estimate was recoverable. The accessible 300-killed figure refers to losses in the ZAA/terrorist organization and may include non-armed FLN apparatus.
uncertainty_note: Leaving null is more defensible than silently allocating organization losses to combatants.
quality_note: Core numerator unavailable under strict status rules.

formula_note: No explicit Jan-Oct 1957 Algiers civilian direct-death series was recovered. Attack-victim counts are mixed-status and disappearances are not explicit death counts.
uncertainty_note: Core civilian numerator left null rather than converting disappearances into deaths or allocating mixed-status attack victims.
quality_note: Best-known public figures do not satisfy the project’s direct-civilian-death rule.

formula_note: No episode-exact, status-agnostic direct-death total was isolated for Jan-Oct 1957 alone.
uncertainty_note: The 314-dead tally is retained at claim level as broader mixed-status context only.

formula_note: Low = 9 × (4,600 paratroopers + 1,200 armed ALN). Best = 9 × (4,600 paratroopers + 1,000 municipal police + 1,200 armed ALN). High = 9 × (10,000 soldiers in sector Alger-Sahel + 1,200 armed ALN).
uncertainty_note: High bound uses broader sector Alger-Sahel soldiers and still omits some police/gendarmerie/CRS/territorial units because no exact accessible count was recovered from the same source path.
quality_note: Useful denominator bracket, but wide because city versus sector geometry differs.

uncertainty_note: Census-base populations from 1954 are used as exposure bounds because no reliable monthly 1957 Algiers population and displacement/inflow series was recovered.
quality_note: Subnational and defensible, but still static rather than fully dynamic.
